hours_formula_detail: Iowa applies the flat federal ABAWD work standard of 80 actual hours per month (about 20 hours/week). The current policy manual — Iowa HHS Employees' Manual, Title 7, Chapter I, 'ABAWD Work Requirement' (revised February 13, 2026, issued via General Letter No. 7-I-54) — states verbatim: 'The minimum number of hours required for any of the activities is 80 per month. To be eligible beyond the limits, ABAWDs must be: Working a total of 80 or more actual hours in a month. Do not use minimum wage criteria to determine the number of hours worked.' The explicit instruction NOT to use minimum-wage criteria definitively rules out the workfare allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula for general volunteering. Unpaid/volunteer work counts at actual hours toward the 80-hour standard, not via any allotment math. OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025) did not change the 80-hour figure; it expanded the population subject to the requirement (age band raised to 18-64) and curtailed waivers.
minimum_wage_detail: Not used in the hours computation: Iowa uses the flat 80-hour standard and the manual explicitly instructs caseworkers, 'Do not use minimum wage criteria to determine the number of hours worked.' Iowa has no higher state minimum wage; the federal floor of $7.25/hr applies in-state for any context that references minimum wage, but it does not enter the ABAWD hours calculation. Per instructions, minimumWageUsd is set to 0 for a federal_80 classification. (Note: some Iowa counties have set local minimum-wage recommendations, e.g., Johnson County, but Iowa law preempts enforceable local minimum wages and none affect the ABAWD standard.)
qualifying_org_definition: Per Title 7, Chapter I 'Definition of Work': '“Work” for the purposes of meeting the ABAWD work requirement means: ... Unpaid or volunteer work. This type of work must be performed through an organization (including religious organizations) or be court-ordered community service. One individual performing free labor for another (such as mowing a neighbor's lawn) does not meet the definition of work for this purpose.' No 501(c)(3) status, MOU, EDP, or provider registration is required; the only structural requirement is that the work be channeled through an organization (or be court-ordered community service) rather than informal one-on-one labor.
qualifyingOrgShort: Any organization (including religious organizations) through which unpaid/volunteer work is performed, or court-ordered community service; no 501(c)(3), registration, or provider-approval requirement — the only limit is that informal one-on-one free labor (e.g., mowing a neighbor's lawn) does not count.
verification_instrument: No named state form for volunteer-hour verification. Per Title 7, Chapter I 'Reporting and Verifying Hours': 'Use any reasonable means to determine and verify the actual number of hours worked in a calendar month.' Caseworker discretion governs; once hours are verified, 'it is not necessary to do so each month' unless the recipient reports hours dropping below 80. Form 470-5674 ('SNAP Work Rules', Rev. 10/25) is a recipient NOTICE letter that explains the rules — it is NOT a hours-verification submission instrument.
remote_stance_detail: Iowa's Definition of Work requires only that unpaid/volunteer work be performed 'through an organization'; the manual is silent on whether the work must be in-person or may be remote/virtual. No Iowa source addresses remote volunteering for ABAWD purposes. Operationally permissible under the federal default, but not affirmatively confirmed — hence 'silent'.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Iowa's Definition of Work (Title 7, Chapter I) requires only that volunteer work be performed 'through an organization (including religious organizations)' — it imposes no geographic restriction on where the organization is incorporated or based, and Iowa policy is otherwise silent on org location. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying organization suffices absent a state restriction, so a CA-based 501(c)(3) should be accepted operationally, but no Iowa source affirmatively states this — hence 'silent'.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide. Iowa holds no active ABAWD time-limit waivers for FY2026 (Oct 2025-Sep 2026), and no county or area waivers. The recipient notice (470-5674, Rev. 10/25) confirms active enforcement, telling ABAWDs their 3-month clock runs and benefits 'will lose ... until December 2026' (the 36-month period reset). OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025) tightened waiver criteria; Iowa's statewide unemployment is well below the >10% threshold and no Iowa ABAWD waiver is in effect. (Iowa does hold a separate SNAP food-RESTRICTION waiver effective Jan 1, 2026 restricting purchase of certain foods — unrelated to the ABAWD time limit.)
geographicWaivers: None active in FY2026. Iowa is fully enforced statewide with zero geographic/county ABAWD time-limit waivers. OBBBA (2025) further restricted area-waiver eligibility; Iowa unemployment is below the 10% threshold. The only active Iowa SNAP waiver is an unrelated food-restriction (junk-food) waiver.
submissionMechanism: No online portal or named form for reporting volunteer hours. Hours are verified by the caseworker using 'any reasonable means'; recipients report changes by phone to Iowa HHS (1-877-347-5678) or to their county HHS office / eligibility worker. Once verified, hours are presumed to continue unless the recipient reports a drop below 80.
reportingCadence: Change-triggered, not a fixed monthly submission. Once hours are verified, the caseworker does not re-verify each month and assumes the ABAWD continues working 80+ hours unless the recipient reports otherwise. A drop below 80 hours must be reported by the 10th of the month after the change occurs (per form 470-5674 and Chapter I).
submission_deadline: Report changes (hours dropping below 80) by the 10th day of the month after the change occurs (form 470-5674, Rev. 10/25: 'If you are working and your work hours drop below 80 hours a month, you must call us at 1-877-347-5678 by the 10th day of the month after the change occurs.'). Once hours are initially verified, no monthly re-verification is required unless a change is reported.
submissionDeadline: Report a drop below 80 hours by the 10th of the month following the change; no monthly re-verification once hours are initially verified.
age_band_note: OBBBA expanded the ABAWD age band to 18-64. Iowa's recipient notice form 470-5674 (Rev. 10/25) reflects this: 'These requirements apply to you because: You are between ages 18 and 64.' NOTE: the Title 7 Chapter I 'ABAWD Exemptions' pages (last substantively revised for ABAWD content earlier) still list 'Under age 18' and 'Aged 65 or over' as exemptions; the Feb 13, 2026 General Letter 7-I-54 that produced the current chapter PDF was an alien-eligibility revision and did not re-issue the ABAWD exemption pages, so the manual's exemption list lags the federally-mandated 18-64 band shown on the current recipient notice. The operative enforced band for FY2026 is 18-64.
notes:
VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Confirmed the prior federal_80 classification directly against the CURRENT primary source. The authoritative document is now Iowa HHS Employees' Manual, Title 7, Chapter I, revised February 13, 2026 (General Letter No. 7-I-54, hhs.iowa.gov/media/18190), which supersedes the May 17, 2024 version (GL 7-I-48, media/13477) the prior record cited. Extracted exact language from both via PyMuPDF.

KEY CONFIRMED QUOTES (Chapter I, rev. 2026-02-13):
• ABAWD Work Requirement: 'The minimum number of hours required for any of the activities is 80 per month... Working a total of 80 or more actual hours in a month. Do not use minimum wage criteria to determine the number of hours worked.' → federal_80, NOT a workfare/allotment formula.
• Definition of Work: 'Unpaid or volunteer work. This type of work must be performed through an organization (including religious organizations) or be court-ordered community service. One individual performing free labor for another (such as mowing a neighbor's lawn) does not meet the definition of work for this purpose.' → general self-initiated nonprofit volunteer lane; no registration/EDP/provider-approval prestep.
• Reporting and Verifying Hours: 'Use any reasonable means to determine and verify the actual number of hours worked in a calendar month.' + 'Once you have verified the hours of work, it is not necessary to do so each month. Assume that an ABAWD is continuing to work 80 or more hours monthly unless the ABAWD reports that the hours of work have dropped below 80.' → no named verification form; caseworker discretion; self-reported.
• The chapter contains NO occurrence of the word 'workfare' and no separate registered-provider lane. Qualifying work PROGRAMS (WIOA Title I, Trade Act §236, state/political-subdivision E&T including SNAP E&T) are listed as a SEPARATE qualifying activity from the volunteer lane; SNAP E&T in Iowa is explicitly voluntary.

FORM 470-5674 (Rev. 10/25, hhs.iowa.gov/media/6238) is a recipient NOTICE letter (not a verification submission form). It confirms: 80 hours/month; 'Volunteering' listed as a qualifying activity; OBBBA age band 'between ages 18 and 64'; and the change-reporting deadline: 'If ... your work hours drop below 80 hours a month, you must call us at 1-877-347-5678 by the 10th day of the month after the change occurs.' It also states ABAWDs who exhaust their 3 months 'will lose your SNAP benefits until December 2026,' confirming active statewide enforcement.

WAIVERS: Iowa has no active ABAWD time-limit waiver for FY2026 (FNS waiver lists; corroborated by Ballotpedia and benefit screeners). Iowa's only current SNAP waiver is an unrelated food-RESTRICTION (junk-food) waiver effective Jan 1, 2026.

AGE-BAND NUANCE: The current recipient notice reflects OBBBA's 18-64 band, but the Chapter I exemption pages still text 'Under age 18 / Aged 65 or over' because the Feb 2026 GL was an alien-eligibility revision that did not re-issue the ABAWD exemption pages. Flagged in 'unresolved'. Does not affect classification or the 80-hour figure.

CONFIDENCE: high — classification (federal_80), hours (80/mo), volunteer lane, no-named-form, and no-waiver are all directly confirmed by the current state policy manual and recipient notice.
unresolved: No named state form exists for org/recipient certification of volunteer hours; verification is by caseworker 'any reasonable means', so verificationFormName remains 'unknown'.
Remote/virtual volunteering: Iowa manual is silent on whether volunteer work must be in-person; not affirmatively addressed by any Iowa source.
Out-of-state 501(c)(3): Iowa imposes no in-state org requirement but does not affirmatively confirm out-of-state nonprofits; 'silent'.

Age-band lag: the current Chapter I (rev. 2026-02-13) exemption pages still list under-18/65+ exemptions and do not text the OBBBA 18-64 band, whereas the recipient notice 470-5674 (Rev. 10/25) does. The Feb 2026 General Letter was an alien-eligibility revision; an updated ABAWD-exemption page reflecting 18-64 was not located. Enforced band for FY2026 is 18-64.
